mission.title: Install and Configure Knative Serving on Kubernetes
mission.description: Knative Serving is a Kubernetes-based platform that enables the deployment and serving of applications and functions as serverless containers. It allows for rapid deployment, automatic scaling, and advanced routing capabilities, making it ideal for modern cloud-native applications. Knative is installed via kubectl apply with YAML manifests from the official GitHub releases.

steps.title: Install Knative Serving CRDs
steps.description:
Apply the Knative Serving Custom Resource Definitions (CRDs) from the official release:

```bash
kubectl apply -f https://github.com/knative/serving/releases/download/knative-v1.21.1/serving-crds.yaml
```

steps.title: Install Knative Serving Core
steps.description:
Apply the core Knative Serving components. This creates the knative-serving namespace and deploys the controller, webhook, and other core services:

```bash
kubectl apply -f https://github.com/knative/serving/releases/download/knative-v1.21.1/serving-core.yaml
```

steps.title: Install a networking layer
steps.description:
Knative requires a networking layer. Choose one of the following options:

**Option A: Kourier (lightweight, recommended for getting started)**
```bash
kubectl apply -f https://github.com/knative-extensions/net-kourier/releases/download/knative-v1.21.0/kourier.yaml
```
Then configure Knative to use Kourier:
```bash
kubectl patch configmap/config-network \
  --namespace knative-serving \
  --type merge \
  --patch '{"data":{"ingress-class":"kourier.ingress.networking.knative.dev"}}'
```

**Option B: Istio**
```bash
kubectl apply -f https://github.com/knative-extensions/net-istio/releases/download/knative-v1.21.1/istio.yaml
kubectl apply -f https://github.com/knative-extensions/net-istio/releases/download/knative-v1.21.1/net-istio.yaml
```

**Option C: Contour**
```bash
kubectl apply -f https://github.com/knative-extensions/net-contour/releases/download/knative-v1.21.1/contour.yaml
kubectl apply -f https://github.com/knative-extensions/net-contour/releases/download/knative-v1.21.1/net-contour.yaml
```

steps.title: Verify the installation
steps.description:
Check that all Knative Serving components are running in the knative-serving namespace:

```bash
kubectl get pods --namespace knative-serving
```

All pods should be in the Running state.

steps.title: Configure DNS (optional)
steps.description:
Optionally install the default domain configuration for development:

```bash
kubectl apply -f https://github.com/knative/serving/releases/download/knative-v1.21.1/serving-default-domain.yaml
```

For production, configure a real DNS domain in the config-domain ConfigMap.

uninstall.title: Remove the networking layer
uninstall.description:
Delete the networking layer you installed (example for Kourier):

```bash
kubectl delete -f https://github.com/knative-extensions/net-kourier/releases/download/knative-v1.21.0/kourier.yaml
```

uninstall.title: Remove Knative Serving core
uninstall.description:
Delete the Knative Serving core components:

```bash
kubectl delete -f https://github.com/knative/serving/releases/download/knative-v1.21.1/serving-core.yaml
```

uninstall.title: Remove Knative Serving CRDs
uninstall.description:
Delete the Custom Resource Definitions:

```bash
kubectl delete -f https://github.com/knative/serving/releases/download/knative-v1.21.1/serving-crds.yaml
```

upgrade.title: Upgrade Knative Serving
upgrade.description:
To upgrade, apply the new version manifests in order. Replace the version in the URLs:

```bash
kubectl apply -f https://github.com/knative/serving/releases/download/knative-v<NEW_VERSION>/serving-crds.yaml
kubectl apply -f https://github.com/knative/serving/releases/download/knative-v<NEW_VERSION>/serving-core.yaml
```

Then upgrade your networking layer similarly.

upgrade.title: Verify the upgrade
upgrade.description:
Check that the upgraded components are running:

```bash
kubectl get pods --namespace knative-serving
```

troubleshooting.title: Pods stuck in Pending state
troubleshooting.description:
If pods are stuck in Pending, check for insufficient resources or node issues:

```bash
kubectl describe pod <pod-name> --namespace knative-serving
```

troubleshooting.title: Knative services not routing traffic
troubleshooting.description:
Verify that the networking layer is correctly configured and the ingress class matches:

```bash
kubectl get ksvc --all-namespaces
kubectl get configmap config-network -n knative-serving -o yaml
```

troubleshooting.title: Controller pods in CrashLoopBackOff
troubleshooting.description:
Check the logs of the controller pod for errors:

```bash
kubectl logs -l app=controller --namespace knative-serving
```

resolution.summary: A successful installation of Knative Serving will have all components running in the knative-serving namespace, including the activator, autoscaler, controller, and webhook pods. You will be able to deploy Knative Services (ksvc) that automatically scale to zero.

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a Kubernetes cluster running version 1.33 or higher (required by Knative 1.21+), and that kubectl is installed and configured to interact with your cluster.
